status_text:
M42 Data Platform — weekly status, week ending May 23, 2026. Reporter: Maria Hernandez.

This week we shipped the v2.3 query optimizer release on schedule (May 21). Initial production metrics show query latency p95 down 18% week-over-week, exceeding our 12% target. Adoption: 47 of 60 internal teams have migrated, on track for 100% by June 6.

Tasks completed:
- Olivia deployed the v2.3 optimizer to production on May 21 (DONE).
- Tariq published the migration guide and ran 3 office hours sessions (DONE).

Tasks in flight:
- Jin will complete the audit log refactor by June 4. Currently 70% done based on PR review status — on track.
- Aisha is leading the SSO integration with M42 Auth, blocked on receiving the OAuth client config from the M42 Auth team. Expected unblocked by May 28; if not, we'll escalate to Dr. Khalid.

KPIs:
- Query latency p95: 142ms, down 18% vs week ending May 16.
- Internal team adoption: 78% (47/60), up from 65% last week.
- Open P1 bugs: 0.
- Customer satisfaction (internal NPS): 62, up 8 points vs Q1 baseline.

Risks: One risk worth flagging — the SSO dependency on M42 Auth. If we slip past May 28, we'd defer the v2.4 release from June 15 to June 22, potential impact ~$80K in deferred internal-team productivity.

Decisions next week: whether to expand the v2.3 query optimizer to external customers (currently internal-only). Tariq is preparing a recommendation.
